title: RBI Revamps Norms for Forex Dealers; Halts New Money Changer Licences (May 06, 2026)
content:
# RBI Revamps Norms for Forex Dealers; Halts New Money Changer Licences (May 06, 2026)

Ek important update hai authorized persons and foreign exchange dealers ke liye! Reserve Bank of India (RBI) ne foreign exchange transactions ko regulate karne ke liye naye norms announce kiye hain. Yeh changes Foreign Exchange Management (Authorised Persons) Regulations, 2026 ke through laye gaye hain.

Here's a quick lowdown on what's new:

*   **New Regulations Introduced:** The RBI has notified the **Foreign Exchange Management (Authorised Persons) Regulations, 2026**. These regulations, issued vide Notification No. FEMA 401/2026-RB dated April 30, 2026, were published in the Official Gazette on May 06, 2026. A.P. (DIR Series) Circular No. 09, dated May 06, 2026, further details these changes.
*   **No New FFMC Licences:** Ab se, RBI Full-Fledged Money Changers (FFMCs) ke liye fresh authorisation applications consider nahi karega, except those already in process. This move aims to streamline the authorization framework.
*   **Rationalisation of Framework:** The new regulations intend to rationalize the authorization and renewal framework for Authorized Persons. Iska objective foreign exchange services ki delivery ko improve karna aur compliance requirements ko easy banana hai.
*   **Principal-Agent Model & Franchisee Discontinuation:** The framework extends the principal-agent model for foreign exchange facility delivery. Also, existing franchisee arrangements within the money changing business will be phased out within two years, with no new arrangements permitted.
*   **New Category Introduced:** A new Authorised Dealer Category-III has been introduced for entities whose foreign exchange dealings are incidental to their core business or who offer innovative forex-linked products.
*   **Minimum Net Worth Requirements:** New and existing authorized persons will need to meet specified minimum net worth requirements, such as ₹10 crore for AD Category-II entities and ₹2 crore for AD Category-III entities.

## Read the Official Document
*   [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.rbi.org.in/Scripts/NotificationUser.aspx)

*Published on: 8 May 2026*
